title: USNS Mercy hospital ship conducts medical engagement in Philippines as part of Pacific Partnership 2026
summary: Military Sealift Command reported USNS Mercy completed a five-day medical engagement in Legazpi, Philippines, treating over 3,200 patients and conducting surgical procedures as part of the Pacific Partnership 2026 humanitarian mission.
contents:
# USNS Mercy Completes Medical Engagement in Philippines

## Mission Update
Military Sealift Command reported that USNS Mercy (T-AH 19) completed a five-day medical engagement in Legazpi, Albay Province, Philippines, as part of Pacific Partnership 2026.

## Medical Operations Summary
- 3,247 patients treated across multiple specialties
- 89 surgical procedures completed (ophthalmology, general surgery, orthopedics)
- 1,200 dental treatments provided
- Medical training conducted for 150 Filipino healthcare workers
- Veterinary services reached 400+ animals in surrounding communities

## Partner Participation
- US Navy medical personnel and civilian mariners
- Philippine Navy medical teams
- Royal Australian Navy medical detachment
- Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force observers
- NGO partners providing specialized care

## Next Port of Call
USNS Mercy departed Legazpi and set course for Vietnam, the next scheduled stop on the five-month Pacific Partnership deployment supporting INDOPACOM theater security cooperation.

## Strategic Significance
Pacific Partnership represents the largest annual multinational humanitarian assistance mission in the Indo-Pacific, strengthening interoperability and building partner capacity across the region.
